title: drosera australis
intro: Drosera australis, also known as the Australian sundew, is a carnivorous plant species native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its leaves covered with sticky droplets that capture small insects for nutrition. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Drosera australis include soothing respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and alleviating symptoms of bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Drosera australis can be found in the form of tinctures, infusions, and capsules, which can be used to support respiratory health and relieve digestive issues.
